name: EDL-003 Suspicious Sudo Privilege Escalation
description: Detects sudo activity associated with privilege escalation abuse: service accounts spawning root shells or reading credential stores (/etc/shadow, su -), and users denied by the sudoers policy ('user NOT in sudoers'). Service accounts should never run interactive root shells.
author: elastic-detection-lab
query: event.dataset:system.auth and event.action:sudo_command and ((user.name:svc_* and process.command_line:("/bin/bash" or "/bin/sh" or *\/etc\/shadow* or *su\ \-\ root*)) or event.outcome:failure)
false_positives: Administrators legitimately using service accounts for break-glass maintenance.
Configuration management tools running shells via sudo (allowlist their command lines).
